quick_facts.label: Conducting body
quick_facts.value: Reserve Bank of India Services Board conducts direct recruitment for Officers in Grade ‘B’ in the RBI’s General, DEPR (Department of Economic and Policy Research), and DSIM (Department of Statistics and Information Management) cadres.

quick_facts.label: Selection stages
quick_facts.value: The 2026 notice specifies Phase-I and Phase-II online/written examinations followed by an interview.

quick_facts.label: General-cadre education requirement (2026 notice)
quick_facts.value: For Grade B (DR)–General, eligibility was graduation with at least 60% aggregate marks, or post-graduation with at least 55%; the notice provides lower thresholds for eligible SC/ST/PwBD applicants where posts are reserved.

quick_facts.label: Age requirement (2026 notice)
quick_facts.value: For the 2026 recruitment, the base age band was 21 to 30 years as on April 1, 2026, with specified upper-age relaxations for eligible categories and circumstances.

quick_facts.label: 2026 application window
quick_facts.value: For panel year 2026, online registration and fee payment opened on April 29, 2026 and closed at 6:00 PM on May 20, 2026. These dates are recruitment-cycle-specific and may change in future notices.

quick_facts.label: 2026 General-cadre fees
quick_facts.value: The 2026 notice listed ₹850 plus 18% GST for GEN/OBC/EWS applicants and ₹100 plus 18% GST as intimation charges for SC/ST/PwBD applicants; RBI staff candidates were listed as nil. Bank/transaction charges were additional, and future fees may differ.

sections.heading: What RBI Grade B is for
paragraphs: RBI Grade B is a direct-recruitment route into officer roles at the Reserve Bank of India. The 2026 recruitment notice covered General, DEPR, and DSIM cadres. RBI describes Grade B work as spanning an array of roles within a full-service central bank, with the precise work depending on posting and cadre.
bullets: The General cadre is the broad officer stream.
DEPR is the economics and policy-research stream.
DSIM is the statistics and information-management stream.

sections.heading: Eligibility: choose the cadre that matches your qualification
paragraphs: Eligibility is set separately for each recruitment cycle and cadre. In the 2026 notice, candidates needed to meet nationality, age, education, and other conditions as of the notice’s specified cutoff date. Applicants should use the current RBI notice rather than relying on an earlier year’s thresholds.

For Grade B (DR)–General in 2026, RBI accepted a recognised graduation/equivalent technical or professional qualification with at least 60% aggregate marks, or a recognised post-graduation/equivalent qualification with at least 55%. Eligible SC/ST/PwBD candidates had the relaxations stated in the notice where posts were reserved. DEPR required specified economics or finance master’s qualifications; DSIM required specified statistics, mathematics, econometrics, informatics, data-science, AI, or related qualifications at the master’s level, or an eligible four-year bachelor’s degree in listed fields.
bullets: 2026 base age band: 21–30 years as of April 1, 2026; the notice listed category-, qualification-, and experience-based upper-age relaxations.
For General/EWS candidates, the 2026 notice barred applying after six prior Phase-I appearances; it stated that this restriction did not apply to SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates where posts were reserved.
Candidates could apply for either DEPR or DSIM, according to the 2026 notice; applicants should confirm the choices available in the relevant year’s advertisement.

sections.heading: Selection format
paragraphs: RBI’s 2026 scheme provides for Phase-I and Phase-II examinations, conducted online and/or in written mode depending on cadre, followed by an interview. Progression is sequential: candidates must be shortlisted at the earlier stage to advance.

For the General cadre, RBI’s official 2024 Phase-II result notice identifies the three Phase-II papers as Economic and Social Issues, English (Writing Skills), and General Finance and Management. Candidates should treat the current recruitment advertisement and its appendices as controlling, because scheduling, modality, and other details may be revised from one panel year to another.
bullets: Panel year 2026 scheduled General Phase-I for June 13, 2026 and General Phase-II for July 25, 2026.
For panel year 2026, DEPR Phase-I and DSIM Phase-I were scheduled for June 14, 2026; their Phase-II examinations were scheduled for July 26, 2026.
RBI states that candidates must appear in all required Phase-II papers and shifts.

sections.heading: How to apply
paragraphs: Applications are submitted online only through RBI’s website; the 2026 notice states that no other submission mode is available. Applicants should first verify their cadre-specific eligibility, complete the online application, upload/provide the required information and documents as directed, pay the applicable fee or intimation charge, and preserve the registration details and application record.

Eligibility is finally checked by the RBI Services Board at the interview stage. The notice warns that incorrect information or failure to meet the conditions can result in cancellation of candidature, including after provisional admission to examinations. RBI also says corrigenda, if issued, will be published on its website.
bullets: For the 2026 cycle, the application window was April 29–May 20, 2026, and is now closed as of July 19, 2026.
The 2026 notice provided a two-day post-closing edit window for limited fields; future edit-window availability and fields are not guaranteed.
Candidates employed in government, public-sector, or similar organisations must follow the employer-intimation requirements set out in the applicable notice.

sections.heading: Appointment and career context
paragraphs: Successful candidates are appointed as Officers in Grade B in the cadre for which they are selected. The 2026 notice states that General-stream selectees join the General Cadre, while DEPR and DSIM selectees join their respective cadres. Initial appointment is on probation for two years, which RBI may extend up to four years, and selected candidates are liable to be posted and transferred anywhere in India.

Compensation is variable and should be read as dated context rather than a guaranteed future figure. The April 2026 notice listed starting basic pay of ₹78,450 per month in the stated Grade B scale and approximately ₹154,936 initial monthly gross emoluments without HRA, alongside allowances and benefits subject to rules in force. It also stated that defined-contribution NPS applies to employees joining RBI on or after January 1, 2012, in addition to gratuity.
bullets: RBI lists prospects for promotion to higher grades.
Accommodation is subject to availability; the 2026 notice stated that HRA at 15% of pay would be paid if accommodation was not provided.
Benefits, allowances, posting conditions, vacancies, and pay can change under RBI rules and future recruitment notices.
